title: Method for facilitating movement of a deadload during ramp operations and smart glass thereof
abstract: According to various embodiments, there is provided a method for facilitating movement of a deadload during ramp operations comprising: identifying a current location of a deadload; identifying a desired location of the deadload; and highlighting both the current location and the desired location of the deadload using a display of a smart glass.
claims:
Claims 1 . A method for facilitating movement of a deadload during ramp operations comprising: identifying a current location of a deadload; identifying a desired location of the deadload; and highlighting both the current location and the desired location of the deadload using a display of a smart glass. 2. The method in accordance with claim 1 , wherein the identifying the current location of the deadload comprises identifying the current location of the deadload by receiving one or more signals from one or more transmitters attached to the deadload. 3. The method in accordance with claim 2, wherein the identifying the current location of the deadload comprises identifying the current location of the deadload based on an optical recognition of the deadload using an optical sensor of the smart glass. 4. The method in accordance with claim 1 , wherein the identifying the desired location of the deadload comprises identifying the desired location of the deadload based on information received from a central server. 5. The method in accordance with claim 1 , wherein the display is a see- through display and wherein the highlighting both the current location and the desired location of the deadload comprises overlaying highlights of the current location and the desired location of the deadload in the see-through display. 6. The method in accordance with claim 3, wherein highlighting both the current location and the desired location of the deadload comprises displaying images captured by the optical sensor of the smart glass with highlight of the current location and the desired location of the deadload. 7. The method in accordance with claim 1 , further comprising receiving detailed information of the deadload, and showing the detailed information of the deadload on the display of the smart glass. 8. The method in accordance with claim 1 , further comprising receiving detailed information of the deadload, and overlaying the detailed information of the deadload on the position of the deadload on the display. 9. The method in accordance with claim 1 , wherein the highlighting both the current location and the desired location of the deadload comprises highlighting current location of two or more deadloads and the desired location corresponding to each of the two or more deadloads to display a visual link between the current location and the desired location of each of the two or more deadloads. 10. The method in accordance with claim 9, further comprising determining the order of movement of deadload among the two or more deadloads. 1 1 . A smart glass for facilitating movement of a deadload during ramp operations, comprising: a location identifier for identifying current location and desired location of the deadload; a display for highlighting the current location and desired location of the deadload; a processor for receiving location information of the deadload from the location identifier and instructing the display to highlighting both the current location and the desired location of the deadload based on the location information. 12. The smart glass in accordance with claim 1 1 , wherein the location identifier is a receiver for receiving signals from one or more transmitters attached to the deadload to identify the current location of the deadload. 13. The smart glass in accordance with claim 1 1 , wherein the smart glass is coupled to a central server that records and stores the desired location of the deadload and wherein the location identifier is a receiver for receiving information on the desired location of the deadload from a central server. 14. The smart glass in accordance with claim 1 1 , wherein the location identifier comprises an optical sensor for identifying current location of the deadload based on optical recognition. 15. The smart glass in accordance with claim 1 1 , wherein the display is a see- through display comprising a transparent screen and the processor is configured to 

 instruct the display to overlay highlights on the current location and the desired location of the deadload in the see-through display of the smart glass. 16. The smart glass in accordance with claim 14, wherein the display is configured to show images captured by the optical sensor of the smart glass with highlights of the current location and the desired location of the deadload. 17. The smart glass in accordance with claim 1 1 , wherein the processor is configured to receive detailed information of the deadload from a central server and instruct the display to show the detailed information of the deadload. 18. The smart glass in accordance with claim 1 1 , wherein the display is configured to overlay the detailed information of the deadload on the position of the deadload. 19. The smart glass in accordance with claim 1 1 , wherein the processor is configured to receive location information of two or more deadloads and to instruct the display to highlight the current location of two or more deadloads and the desired location corresponding to each of the two or more deadloads to display a visual link between the current location and the desired location of each of the two or more deadloads. 20. The smart glass in accordance with claim 19, wherein the processor is configured to determine order of movement of deadload among the two or more deadloads. 21 . A computer readable medium comprising instructions which, when executed by a processor, makes the processor perform a method for facilitating movement of a deadload during ramp operations comprising: receiving location information of a deadload from a location identifier and instructing a display of a smart glass to highlight both the current location and the desired location of the deadload based on the location information.
